content:
_Just write it down before we forget ;-), mostly brought up by Kent Oversteen in the meeting of September_

Usage of external dependencies/code

  • syn
  • code/library can be shared between user space and kernel
  • Linux user space tools written in Rust

Potential solutions

  • User space tools can use cargo, so no problem.
  • git subtree, suggested by Gary Guo
  • use cargo purely for dependencies resolution, i.e. not for building, suggested by Kent Overstreet
    • No idea whether this is possible

Problems questions

  • Need to teach Linus to use git substree? I attempted to ask in the meeting ;-)
  • cargo doesn't seem to support modifying the dependency graph for -Zbuild-std. https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust/rust/Makefile#L190, brought up by Miguel.
Also, if we end up using cargo, will cargo be able to instruct gcc-rs to work (only really of interest, if we use cargo for building.)

Also I know cargo-vendor adds checksum files to the generated output, but I'm currently not sure where this checksum is checked when using the vendor. Is it only used by cargo, so using rustc directly ignores the checksum?

The problem of using cargo in kernel (from my understanding):

  • cargo still doesn't work very well for "freestanding" targets (e.g. OS kernel), maybe [build scripts]https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/build-scripts.html) can help?
  • "supply chain attack" or in other words, the endless fear of kernel developers from the extenal code ;-)

"supply chain attack" or in other words, the endless fear of kernel developers from the extenal code ;-)

For this cargo supports both path dependencies (using the path key when specifying a dependency) and vendoring (using cargo vendor). Mozilla also recently released cargo-vet for help with auditing dependencies. Whether that will be considered enough is another question. It should also be possible to replace the crates-io registry with an empty registry to prevent having any dependencies from crates.io.

cargo still doesn't work very well for "freestanding" targets (e.g. OS kernel), maybe [build scripts]https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/build-scripts.html) can help?

Build scripts don't help with freestanding targets.

use cargo purely for dependencies resolution, i.e. not for building, suggested by Kent Overstreet

It is possible using cargo metadata, but getting eg build scripts working is non-trivial I believe. This is the method used by for example Bazel though.
